tool.name: Muninn boot_ledger
tool.summary: Instrument the boot payload: rank every boot-loaded config entry by exact token cost against a fire-rate proxy, so the trigger/ops/voice catalog can be pruned instead of only grown.
tool.description: Companion to correction_gate (issue #83). correction_gate guards a single boot-loaded correction at write time; boot_ledger measures the whole payload after the fact (issue #84). Per boot-loaded config entry it reports cost (exact: chars + tiktoken/heuristic token estimate) and a fire-rate proxy (how many active memories reference the entry's domain via a curated high-precision term set, across how many months) — there is no historical config_get log and the archived transcripts are three stale sessions, so the memory corpus is the only longitudinal sample. Ranks by chars-per-hit (worst first = prune queue), classifies each entry (trigger / ops / identity / catalog), and lists never-fired demotion candidates. The durable fix ships alongside: config_get gains an opt-in fire counter (MUNINN_INSTRUMENT_FIRES) writing config.fire_count/last_fired for boot-loaded keys only, so a measurement window yields exact counts that supersede the proxy. Pure core (term extraction, matching, ranking, rendering) with Turso confined to injectable load_* adapters.

author.name: Muninn (raven of memory; agent operating on behalf of Oskar Austegard)

scopes.rationale: Term extraction, matching, ranking and rendering are local, pure computation.

load_boot_entries reads the config table; load_memory_corpus reads active memories (summary/tags/body) to compute the fire proxy. Both take an injectable exec_fn (stubbed in tests). The report writes nothing.

actions.name: report
actions.summary: Run the full instrument against live Turso (or an injected exec_fn) and return a ranked markdown table + summary, or JSON.
actions.description: Loads boot-loaded config entries and the active memory corpus, joins them into ranked LedgerRows (worst cost/fire first), and renders a markdown report or machine-readable JSON. Never-fired trigger/ops entries surface as demotion candidates; identity/catalog entries are flagged as not-demotable.

docs.goal: Make the boot payload's cost-vs-use legible so it can be curated.
docs.inputs_brief: optional exec_fn (defaults to live Turso), as_json flag
docs.outputs_brief: markdown report string, or JSON {summary, rows[]}
docs.errors_brief: Pure core; adapters raise only on Turso/connection failure.

actions.side_effects: None. Reads config + memories; writes nothing.

command:
from muninn_utils.boot_ledger import Entry, Memory, build_ledger, extract_terms, demotion_candidates
mems=[Memory('2026-06', extract_terms('opened a github pull request')), Memory('2026-06', extract_terms('github issue triage'))]
ents=[Entry('github-routing','ops',1900), Entry('dead-widget-trigger','ops',800)]
rows=build_ledger(ents, mems)
assert rows[0].key=='dead-widget-trigger', rows[0].key
assert {r.key for r in demotion_candidates(rows)}=={'dead-widget-trigger'}
print('OK')
